description:
Create a Flower-Insect Implicit Association Test (IAT) in PsychoPy Builder to measure implicit attitudes by comparing reaction times in congruent vs. incongruent pairing blocks. The experiment must be saved to /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/IAT/iat_experiment.psyexp and include an instructions routine followed by five block routines, each using a loop linked to a specific conditions file in /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/IAT/conditions/.

Generate the following CSV conditions files with 'stimulus', 'category', and 'corrAns' columns:
- block1_target_practice.csv (8 rows): Flowers (Rose, Tulip, Daisy, Lily) mapped to 'e'; Insects (Ant, Spider, Beetle, Mosquito) mapped to 'i'.
- block2_attribute_practice.csv (8 rows): Pleasant (Joy, Love, Peace, Happy) mapped to 'e'; Unpleasant (Agony, Terrible, Horrible, Nasty) mapped to 'i'.
- block3_combined_congruent.csv (16 rows): Combined mappings from Blocks 1 & 2.
- block4_reversed_target.csv (8 rows): Insects mapped to 'e'; Flowers mapped to 'i'.
- block5_combined_incongruent.csv (16 rows): Combined mappings from Blocks 4 & 2 (Insects/Pleasant to 'e'; Flowers/Unpleasant to 'i').

The experiment must display the stimulus word with category labels on the top-left and top-right of the screen as reminders of the mapping and record responses using the 'e' and 'i' keys.
